summary: omnius ships a postinstall daemon that profiles the host and beacons over HTTP
details: The package declares both preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks in package.json (around line 73-74) and ships dist/postinstall-daemon.cjs, a CommonJS bundle that on install loads os, fs, http, and child_process and runs synchronous shell commands (execSync) plus repeated ping invocations to gather host identifiers (os.userInfo() and other system info appear at lines 160 and 395). The same module constructs http.request GET calls (around lines 85-87) so the collected host data is sent over the network at install time. package.json line 142 additionally uses curl in lifecycle scripting, and dist/scripts/web_scrape.py contains further ping/wget/POST activity. The combination of preinstall+postinstall hooks, a daemon bundle that fingerprints the host with execSync/os.userInfo and beacons over HTTP, and supporting curl/wget/ping primitives is the canonical install-time host-profiling and beacon shape; running `npm install omnius` causes the installer's machine to execute this code and emit identifying data outbound.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
